definitions.text: A framework for language agents that utilizes verbal reinforcement learning to enhance their performance by allowing agents to receive feedback through language.
Reflexion is a framework designed to enhance the learning process of language agents by utilizing linguistic feedback and storing reflective text in an episodic memory buffer.
A novel approach that utilizes verbal reinforcement to enhance the learning capabilities of autonomous decision-making agents built on large language models.

The Reflexion framework is a system composed of three interconnected models: the Actor, the Evaluator, and the Self-Reflection model, designed to enhance decision-making through iterative learning and feedback.
A method that demonstrates significant improvement over strong baselines in natural language reinforcement learning tasks.
An approach that leverages self-reflection to distill past experiences into useful 'self-hints' for error correction and task improvement.
An approach that incorporates self-reflection to enhance learning efficiency in reasoning tasks.
A method for generating code in programming languages that utilizes self-generated unit test suites to enhance code generation accuracy.
Reflexion is an optimization technique that leverages natural language for policy optimization and enhances action selection through experiential learning.
Reflexion is an approach that uses verbal reinforcement to enable agents to learn from their past mistakes, leading to improved decision-making performance.

selected.text: Reflexion is a framework designed to enhance the learning process of language agents by utilizing linguistic feedback and storing reflective text in an episodic memory buffer.

definitions.text: An approach that enables agents to receive feedback through language to adjust their behavior and improve decision-making processes.
A proposed approach that aims to improve the interpretability and diagnosability of autonomous agents in reinforcement learning.

selected.text: An approach that enables agents to receive feedback through language to adjust their behavior and improve decision-making processes.

definitions.text: The Actor is a model that generates text and actions based on state observations, utilizing a large language model and a memory component for improved context awareness.

selected.text: The Actor is a model that generates text and actions based on state observations, utilizing a large language model and a memory component for improved context awareness.

definitions.text: The Evaluator is a model that assesses the quality of the Actor's outputs by computing reward scores that reflect performance in specific tasks.

selected.text: The Evaluator is a model that assesses the quality of the Actor's outputs by computing reward scores that reflect performance in specific tasks.

definitions.text: The Self-Reflection model generates verbal feedback based on the Actor's performance, providing insights that inform future actions and are stored in memory.

selected.text: The Self-Reflection model generates verbal feedback based on the Actor's performance, providing insights that inform future actions and are stored in memory.
